first_measurements.notes: Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Confirm common ground reference.
Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record no abnormal heating after first power.
Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.
first_measurements.target: 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) supply voltage and polarity: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3

first_measurements.notes: Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.

tasks.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 3.5mm Audio Jack: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map 40-pin GPIO Header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map Display Port (eDP): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map RJ45 Ethernet: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 3.5mm Audio Jack.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO Header.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Display Port (eDP).
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO Header before any external connection.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for Display Port (eDP) before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) ground reference continuity: 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) logic voltage domain: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) signal pin continuity map: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) supply voltage and polarity: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) supply-to-ground no-short: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3 vs 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Record HDMI (hdmi) ground reference continuity: 17:DDC_GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record HDMI (hdmi) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmi) supply voltage and polarity: 18:+5V. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.
Record HDMI (hdmi) supply-to-ground no-short: 18:+5V vs 17:DDC_GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

claim_boundary: This casefile assembles evidence and authority state. It does not turn model output into repair authority; production release requires measured evidence, terminal outcome, and a passing authority ledger.

design_test_kit.claim_boundary: This test kit can validate requirements, module contracts, evidence gates, and supplied DC power-tree netlists. It does not authorize arbitrary unknown boards, mains work, battery packs, or production release without measured topology, bench evidence, and specialist review where hazards apply.

compiled_topology_netlist.claim_boundary: No simulation model was compiled because no usable topology evidence was supplied.

issues.detail: No topology_evidence.v1 or bench_topology_capture.v1 packet was supplied.
issues.summary: Topology netlist compiler did not run.

load_envelope.reason: No topology_evidence.v1 or bench_topology_capture.v1 packet was supplied.

modules.role: Accept a known source or module.
Limit fault current and reverse-polarity mistakes.
Optionally show voltage/current and enable output.

required_measurements: source voltage and current limit
no-short resistance from each rail to ground
load startup current
load steady current
rail voltage under load
thermal behavior after duty-cycle run

test_vectors.purpose: Prove source polarity, rail voltage, and no-short state before integration.
Simulate rail voltage, source current, trace drop, regulator stress, and load draw.
Run the target behavior and record pass/fail output evidence.
Confirm every module edge has named power, ground, signal, connector, pinout, and rating contracts.

next_actions: Close failed module/resource contracts before treating the design as complete.
Fill the simulation fixture with measured source voltage, source current limit, load current, trace/regulator model, and rail constraints.
Write the target output function, pass/fail behavior, operating voltage, and stop conditions.
Draw or record the controller, power, input, output, and connector boundaries.

subject.goal: Understand this physical Raspberry Pi-style board for repair, salvage, reuse, and safe splice/build planning. Identify visible functions and the next measurements needed; do not claim safe power from photos.

tests.rationale: The test kit has a structured project intent, requirements, architecture blocks, and evidence gates to test.
At least one terminal output function and its evidence need are present.
power, connector, protection

forbidden_claims: Board is reusable without further testing.
Exact pinout is known without measurement.
Power rails are identified without continuity check.

measurement_priorities.action: Measure continuity from USB-C VBUS to candidate power rail, and ground to ground plane.
measurement_priorities.expected_evidence: VBUS to rail continuity < 1 ohm, ground continuity < 1 ohm, no short to ground.
measurement_priorities.why: Primary power input; must identify rail and verify no-short before powering.

measurement_priorities.action: Map pinout: identify pin 1, ground pins, 3.3V and 5V supply pins via continuity to known rails.
measurement_priorities.expected_evidence: Pin 1 identified, ground pins continuous to ground plane, 3.3V and 5V pins continuous to respective rails.
measurement_priorities.stop_condition: All supply and ground pins mapped.
measurement_priorities.why: Key interface for repair and salvage; supply pins must be known for safe power.

measurement_priorities.action: Measure unpowered resistance between each supply pin and ground to confirm no short.
measurement_priorities.expected_evidence: Resistance > 1 kohm for all supply pins to ground.
measurement_priorities.stop_condition: All supply pins show > 1 kohm to ground.
measurement_priorities.why: Ensure no short before applying power.

measurement_priorities.action: Map pinout: identify ground and supply pins via continuity to ground plane and power rails.
measurement_priorities.expected_evidence: Ground pins continuous to ground, supply pins (if any) continuous to known rail.
measurement_priorities.stop_condition: All ground and supply pins identified.
measurement_priorities.why: HDMI may carry power; must verify before use.

measurement_priorities.action: Map pinout: identify ground and supply pins via continuity.
measurement_priorities.expected_evidence: Ground pins continuous to ground, supply pins continuous to known rail.
measurement_priorities.stop_condition: All ground and supply pins identified.
measurement_priorities.why: eDP may have power rails; needed for salvage or reuse.

measurement_priorities.action: Map pinout: identify ground and any supply pins via continuity.
measurement_priorities.expected_evidence: Ground pin continuous to ground, no unexpected supply pins.
measurement_priorities.stop_condition: Pinout mapped and no short to ground.
measurement_priorities.why: Audio jack may have bias voltage; verify for safe use.

advisory.operator_prompt: Perform the measurement priorities in order. Start with USB-C power input continuity and no-short, then map GPIO header pinout and measure supply-to-ground resistance. Use a multimeter in continuity and resistance modes. Record all measurements in the structured template. Do not power the board until all no-short checks pass.

qwen_evidence_uses.limitation: Cannot confirm salvageability without visual continuity and thermal integrity checks.
qwen_evidence_uses.use: Identify SoC and DRAM as potential salvage targets with missing evidence.

input voltage and polarity
current draw under current-limited supply
wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function

steps.action: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
steps.expected_result: Every used connector pin has a measured role and voltage/domain where relevant.
steps.fail_branch: Measured pinout is complete.

steps.action: Record passing trusted resistance evidence.
steps.expected_result: No power rail reads as a dead short to ground.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing resistance evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Ground/reference continuity is stable.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing continuity evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted voltage evidence.
steps.expected_result: Voltage and polarity match the measured low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing voltage evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted current evidence.
steps.expected_result: Current draw remains within the configured current limit.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing current evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted thermal evidence.
steps.expected_result: Thermal behavior remains normal through the function test.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing thermal evidence.

steps.action: Record successful first power, thermal behavior, and output_function_verified=true.
steps.expected_result: Successful output function, first power, and thermal outcome are recorded.
steps.fail_branch: Terminal successful outcome is recorded.

steps.action: Record passing ground/reference continuity and connector continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing current draw/current-limit evidence during first power.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing unpowered resistance/no-short evidence between power and ground.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing thermal behavior after first power and output-function test.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing voltage, polarity, and logic-level evidence under current limit.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Measure unpowered resistance between every power rail and ground.
steps.expected_result: No rail reads as a dead short unless the design explicitly expects it.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate the shorted rail before power.

steps.action: Power through a current limit and record input voltage, rail voltage, and polarity.
steps.expected_result: Rails match the measured or expected low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; inspect regulator/input protection path.

steps.action: Record thermal behavior during first-power and function test.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot, smoke, odor, or thermal runaway.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate load, regulator, or shorted component.

steps.action: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
steps.expected_result: Measured topology_evidence.v1 row is attached, or a negative result is recorded explicitly.
steps.fail_branch: Keep the candidate visual topology outside the splice/power trust boundary.

steps.action: Map 3.5mm Audio Jack: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
steps.expected_result: Measured topology_evidence.v1 row is attached, or a negative result is recorded explicitly.
steps.fail_branch: Keep the candidate visual topology outside the splice/power trust boundary.

steps.action: Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 3.5mm Audio Jack.
steps.expected_result: Measured topology_evidence.v1 row is attached, or a negative result is recorded explicitly.
steps.fail_branch: Keep the candidate visual topology outside the splice/power trust boundary.

steps.action: Map 40-pin GPIO Header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.

steps.fail_branch: Keep the candidate visual topology outside the splice/power trust boundary.

steps.action: Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO Header.
steps.expected_result: Measured topology_evidence.v1 row is attached, or a negative result is recorded explicitly.
steps.fail_branch: Keep the candidate visual topology outside the splice/power trust boundary.

steps.action: Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO Header before any external connection.
steps.expected_result: Measured topology_evidence.v1 row is attached, or a negative result is recorded explicitly.
steps.fail_branch: Keep the candidate visual topology outside the splice/power trust boundary.

resource_strategy.goal: Understand this physical Raspberry Pi-style board for repair, salvage, reuse, and safe splice/build planning. Identify visible functions and the next measurements needed; do not claim safe power from photos.

build_plan_inputs.goal: Understand this physical Raspberry Pi-style board for repair, salvage, reuse, and safe splice/build planning. Identify visible functions and the next measurements needed; do not claim safe power from photos.

honesty: Catalog costs are rough planning estimates, not live quotes.
A selected salvaged or owned resource is not splice-ready until its evidence gates close.
Do not use blocked battery, mains, high-voltage, or failed-evidence resources as gap fillers.
next_actions: Close missing capabilities by adding inventory, procuring gap-fill modules, or changing scope.
Run the listed bench measurements before generating pin-level splice instructions.

resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.

value_summary.value_logic: Value comes from preserving proven resources and spending only where the capability gap is real.

allowed_actions: Capture markings, connector close-ups, and baseline continuity/no-short measurements.
Prepare candidate splice recipes but keep them gated.
reuse_splice_strategy.best_next_checkpoint: Attach measured connector pinout/topology evidence with no unknown pins.
reuse_splice_strategy.claim_boundary: Splice recipes are templates until connector pinout, voltage/current limits, and terminal outcome evidence are recorded.

acceptance: function is identified and measured
no hard safety blocker remains
prohibitions: Do not power or splice unknown boards from visual evidence alone.
splice_steps: Run no-short, voltage, current, and thermal checks.

measurement_queue.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 3.5mm Audio Jack: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 3.5mm Audio Jack.
Map 40-pin GPIO Header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO Header.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO Header before any external connection.
Map Display Port (eDP): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Display Port (eDP).
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for Display Port (eDP) before any external connection.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Map RJ45 Ethernet: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on RJ45 Ethernet.
Map RJ45 Ethernet port: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on RJ45 Ethernet port.
Map USB-A Port 1 (blue): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A Port 1 (blue).
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A Port 1 (blue) before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A Port 1 (blue) to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-A Port 2 (black): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A Port 2 (black).
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A Port 2 (black) before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A Port 2 (black) to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-A port (blue): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port (blue).
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port (blue) before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A port (blue) to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-C Power In: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-C Power In.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-C Power In before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C Power In to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path System-on-Chip (SoC) to USB-C Power In; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path DRAM chip to HDMI; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path DRAM chip to 3.5mm Audio Jack; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path System-on-Chip (SoC) to HDMI; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path LPDDR4 SDRAM to USB-A Port 1 (blue); record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path SoC (BCM2711) to 40-pin GPIO Header; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path SoC (BCM2711) to Display Port (eDP); record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path LPDDR4 SDRAM to RJ45 Ethernet port; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path DRAM chip to USB-C Power In; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.

capture_prompts: part markings on drivers, regulators, controllers, and sensors
new build wiring before first power-up
measurement_prompts: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
review_prompts: operator confirms voltage/current rating before power
operator records final build outcome and value

recommended_first_splice.reason: No circuit-backed reusable function blocks were supplied.

integration_contract.first_demo: demonstrate the reused block alone before connecting it to another module
integration_contract.output_function: known-voltage breakout with safer connectors and fusing
unknowns_to_close: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground

adapter_circuits.name: buck/boost regulator or protected power breakout
adapter_circuits.use_when: source voltage does not exactly match the salvaged load/module rating

do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
required_measurements: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
input voltage and polarity
current draw under current-limited supply
wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function

stop_conditions: input voltage or polarity cannot be identified
load current exceeds the wire, connector, switch, driver, or supply rating
module overheats, smells, smokes, or pulls unexpected current
battery chemistry/protection is unknown or the pack is damaged

tasks.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 3.5mm Audio Jack: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map 40-pin GPIO Header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map Display Port (eDP): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Map RJ45 Ethernet: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 3.5mm Audio Jack.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO Header.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Display Port (eDP).
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO Header before any external connection.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for Display Port (eDP) before any external connection.
Record connector ground to exposed ground. Confirm common ground reference.
Record current draw under current-limited supply. Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record input voltage and polarity. Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record logic high voltage. Measure the reused signal voltage domain.
Record power to ground no-short. Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Record thermal behavior after first power. Record no abnormal heating after first power.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) ground reference continuity: 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) logic voltage domain: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Measure idle/high logic level before connecting to another controller or adapter.
Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) signal pin continuity map: 3:GPIO2_SDA1, 5:GPIO3_SCL1, 7:GPIO4, 8:GPIO14_TXD0, 10:GPIO15_RXD0, 11:GPIO17, 12:GPIO18_PWM0, 13:GPIO27, 15:GPIO22, 16:GPIO23, +18 more. Map each reusable signal to a labeled pad, connector, or target net before splicing.
Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) supply voltage and polarity: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) supply-to-ground no-short: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3 vs 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Record HDMI (hdmi) ground reference continuity: 17:DDC_GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record HDMI (hdmi) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmi) supply voltage and polarity: 18:+5V. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.
Record HDMI (hdmi) supply-to-ground no-short: 18:+5V vs 17:DDC_GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

acceptance: Board identity is narrowed to a reusable function.

release_artifacts_required: production release manifest for any release claim
bench_protocol_pack.release_boundary: This pack defines what must be proven for a scoped low-voltage release. It cannot authorize battery, mains, high-voltage, laser, or unbounded reuse without a separate specialist authority.
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

salvage_items.recommended_handling: Reuse only after impedance, idle-current, and low-level output checks.

salvage_items.recommended_handling: Harvest or reuse as an entry point after continuity and pin-label confirmation.

salvage_items.recommended_handling: Reuse through mapped pins with current limits and protected controller I/O.

salvage_items.recommended_handling: Harvest or reuse as an entry point after continuity and pin-label confirmation.

evidence: Vision evidence can propose components and connectors but cannot prove pinout.
items.resolution: Capture measured connector pinout, no-short, ground, and voltage evidence.
items.summary: Visual board candidates exist without measured topology.

diagnostic_actions: Run logic-level, shared-ground, loopback, and safe serial-capture steps.
candidates.label: USB/serial bridge, connector, boot, or logic-level fault

diagnostic_actions: Map pins, verify current limits, and test through protected controller I/O.
candidates.label: Display/UI pin map, current limit, or connector fault

diagnostic_actions: Verify load impedance, idle current, low-level output, and thermal behavior.
candidates.label: Audio load, amplifier, connector, or protection fault

allowed_layout_actions: whole-board reuse after authority gates close
connector-based harness reuse after measured pinout
label and fixture known connector entry points
layout_reuse_boundaries.claim_boundary: Layout boundaries are conservative. Without front/back geometry and measured topology, the engine may recommend whole-board or connector reuse, but not board-section cutting or hidden-trace salvage.
missing_layout_evidence: Attach component/connector geometry or annotated regions for layout-aware salvage.
Attach measured pinout/topology before section salvage or external splice.
prohibited_layout_actions: board-section cutting from visual evidence alone
hidden-trace splice or copper cut without backside/topology proof

acceptance: Board identity is narrowed to a reusable function.

release_artifacts_required: production release manifest for any release claim
bench_protocol_pack.release_boundary: This pack defines what must be proven for a scoped low-voltage release. It cannot authorize battery, mains, high-voltage, laser, or unbounded reuse without a separate specialist authority.
required_equipment: current-limited bench supply or protected USB source
setup_controls: Keep unknown boards unpowered until no-short and polarity checks pass.
Use current limits for every first-power or load test.
Record instrument, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI for each passing measurement.
Stop on smoke, odor, abnormal heat, unexpected current, shorted rail, or unstable output.
For BCM2711, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For SBC, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.
For RASPBERRY PI, explicitly verify: power input voltage/current, boot/current behavior, GPIO voltage domain, connector map.

Record 40-pin GPIO Header (gpio) supply-to-ground no-short: 1:3V3, 2:5V, 4:5V, 17:3V3 vs 6:GND, 9:GND, 14:GND, 20:GND, 25:GND, 30:GND, 34:GND, 39:GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
Record HDMI (hdmi) ground reference continuity: 17:DDC_GND. Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
Record HDMI (hdmi) pin-1/orientation confirmation. Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Record HDMI (hdmi) supply voltage and polarity: 18:+5V. Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.
Record HDMI (hdmi) supply-to-ground no-short: 18:+5V vs 17:DDC_GND. Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.

steps.expected_result: Every used connector pin has a measured role and voltage/domain where relevant.
steps.fail_branch: Measured pinout is complete.

steps.action: Record passing trusted resistance evidence.
steps.expected_result: No power rail reads as a dead short to ground.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing resistance evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Ground/reference continuity is stable.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing continuity evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted voltage evidence.
steps.expected_result: Voltage and polarity match the measured low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing voltage evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted current evidence.
steps.expected_result: Current draw remains within the configured current limit.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing current evidence.

steps.action: Record passing trusted thermal evidence.
steps.expected_result: Thermal behavior remains normal through the function test.
steps.fail_branch: Missing passing thermal evidence.

steps.action: Record successful first power, thermal behavior, and output_function_verified=true.
steps.expected_result: Successful output function, first power, and thermal outcome are recorded.
steps.fail_branch: Terminal successful outcome is recorded.

steps.action: Record passing ground/reference continuity and connector continuity evidence.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing current draw/current-limit evidence during first power.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing unpowered resistance/no-short evidence between power and ground.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing thermal behavior after first power and output-function test.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Record passing voltage, polarity, and logic-level evidence under current limit.
steps.expected_result: Evidence passes and is recorded with provenance.
steps.fail_branch: Required by the current repair authority status.

steps.action: Measure unpowered resistance between every power rail and ground.
steps.expected_result: No rail reads as a dead short unless the design explicitly expects it.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate the shorted rail before power.

steps.action: Power through a current limit and record input voltage, rail voltage, and polarity.
steps.expected_result: Rails match the measured or expected low-voltage domain.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; inspect regulator/input protection path.

steps.action: Record thermal behavior during first-power and function test.
steps.expected_result: No abnormal hot spot, smoke, odor, or thermal runaway.
steps.fail_branch: Stop; isolate load, regulator, or shorted component.

steps.action: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
steps.expected_result: Measured topology_evidence.v1 row is attached, or a negative result is recorded explicitly.
steps.fail_branch: Keep the candidate visual topology outside the splice/power trust boundary.

resource_strategy.goal: Understand this physical Raspberry Pi-style board for repair, salvage, reuse, and safe splice/build planning. Identify visible functions and the next measurements needed; do not claim safe power from photos.

build_plan_inputs.goal: Understand this physical Raspberry Pi-style board for repair, salvage, reuse, and safe splice/build planning. Identify visible functions and the next measurements needed; do not claim safe power from photos.

honesty: Catalog costs are rough planning estimates, not live quotes.
A selected salvaged or owned resource is not splice-ready until its evidence gates close.
Do not use blocked battery, mains, high-voltage, or failed-evidence resources as gap fillers.
next_actions: Close missing capabilities by adding inventory, procuring gap-fill modules, or changing scope.
Run the listed bench measurements before generating pin-level splice instructions.

resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.

value_summary.value_logic: Value comes from preserving proven resources and spending only where the capability gap is real.

allowed_actions: Capture markings, connector close-ups, and baseline continuity/no-short measurements.
Prepare candidate splice recipes but keep them gated.

reuse_splice_strategy.claim_boundary: Splice recipes are templates until connector pinout, voltage/current limits, and terminal outcome evidence are recorded.

acceptance: function is identified and measured
no hard safety blocker remains
prohibitions: Do not power or splice unknown boards from visual evidence alone.

mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces

required_measurements: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
input voltage and polarity
current draw under current-limited supply
wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos

mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function

stop_conditions: input voltage or polarity cannot be identified
load current exceeds the wire, connector, switch, driver, or supply rating
module overheats, smells, smokes, or pulls unexpected current
battery chemistry/protection is unknown or the pack is damaged

measurement_queue.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 3.5mm Audio Jack: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 3.5mm Audio Jack.
Map 40-pin GPIO Header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on 40-pin GPIO Header.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for 40-pin GPIO Header before any external connection.
Map Display Port (eDP): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on Display Port (eDP).
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for Display Port (eDP) before any external connection.
Map HDMI: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on HDMI.
Map RJ45 Ethernet: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on RJ45 Ethernet.
Map RJ45 Ethernet port: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on RJ45 Ethernet port.
Map USB-A Port 1 (blue): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A Port 1 (blue).
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A Port 1 (blue) before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A Port 1 (blue) to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-A Port 2 (black): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A Port 2 (black).
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A Port 2 (black) before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A Port 2 (black) to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-A port (blue): pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-A port (blue).
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-A port (blue) before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-A port (blue) to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map USB-C Power In: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-C Power In.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-C Power In before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C Power In to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path System-on-Chip (SoC) to USB-C Power In; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path DRAM chip to HDMI; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path DRAM chip to 3.5mm Audio Jack; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path System-on-Chip (SoC) to HDMI; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path LPDDR4 SDRAM to USB-A Port 1 (blue); record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path SoC (BCM2711) to 40-pin GPIO Header; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path SoC (BCM2711) to Display Port (eDP); record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path LPDDR4 SDRAM to RJ45 Ethernet port; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path DRAM chip to USB-C Power In; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
